title: Judge Me Tender

jokes.text: Ah!
jokes.setup: Opening exclamation in response to unseen stimulus
jokes.explanation: Generic reaction sound with minimal comedic value

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature catchphrase in response to mishap
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfect character integration and cultural impact

jokes.text: Hello, boy. Why, you...!
jokes.setup: Homer greeting then immediately getting frustrated
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer-Bart interaction setup

jokes.text: Ay, caramba!
jokes.setup: Bart's signature exclamation in response to something shocking
jokes.explanation: Bart's established catchphrase - good character integration

jokes.text: I want something that looks like this... had a baby with this, and it got sat on by this.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his ideal ugly dog for the contest
jokes.punchline: The increasingly absurd combination of ugly elements
jokes.explanation: Great escalating structure with vivid absurdist imagery

jokes.text: You never fail to nauseate me, boy. / Just call me 'Barf Simpson.'
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing Bart's nauseating creation
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay with good character dynamic

jokes.text: I wanted to, but your mother said kids might tease you.
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Bart's 'Barf Simpson' suggestion
jokes.punchline: The ironic concern about teasing given their current activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Homer's backwards parenting logic on display

jokes.text: Dad, do you realize we've put more time into this than all my schoolwork combined? / But if we win this, we get a gift certificate. / For what? / It doesn't matter.
jokes.setup: Bart pointing out their misplaced priorities
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete disregard for what the prize actually is
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Homer's illogical priorities

jokes.text: I need a diaper for my chimp that won't upset my tiger's stomach if he eats the chimp.
jokes.setup: Customer making seemingly innocent request at pet store
jokes.punchline: The casual acceptance of tiger eating chimp
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd premise delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Fear not. Our chimp diapers have that nutty cinnamon taste tigers love.
jokes.setup: Following up on the bizarre chimp-tiger scenario
jokes.punchline: The employee having a specific product for this insane need
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurd premise with specific details

jokes.text: Mmm! It's a great time to be a tiger.
jokes.setup: Tiger somehow understanding and commenting on the conversation
jokes.punchline: The tiger's satisfied assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the absurd setup with anthropomorphized animal

jokes.text: So that natural selection was the origin of this species? / Yup, that's exactly... Whoa-oa-oa! You almost got me.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to trap Ned into accepting evolution
jokes.punchline: Ned catching himself before contradicting his beliefs
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work with Lisa's cleverness vs Ned's religious convictions

jokes.text: Not on my watch!
jokes.setup: Ned's determination not to be tricked into accepting evolution
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but fairly predictable

jokes.text: This seat taken? / Uh, yes, I'm saving it for... this soy sauce packet.
jokes.setup: Moe asking about empty seat
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's pathetic excuse with visible prop
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with visible desperation

jokes.text: Aw, man, you know you're a loser when they don't even work hard on the excuse.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy's weak excuse for avoiding Moe
jokes.punchline: Moe's self-aware observation about how pathetic he is
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating observational humor with perfect economy

jokes.text: Thanks a lot, everyone. Choke on yourselves.
jokes.setup: Moe's frustrated response to being rejected
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical but angry parting insult
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific anger with slightly absurd phrasing

jokes.text: Whoa, that one's so ugly, it reminds me of my ex-wife! Hah!
jokes.setup: Krusty commenting on ugly dog
jokes.explanation: Lazy, hacky comedy club material - below par for The Simpsons

jokes.text: That one reminds me of her lawyer. Congress... elderly drivers... commercials... Bella Abzug...
jokes.setup: Krusty continuing his hack observations
jokes.explanation: More hacky material, though the randomness shows it's intentionally bad

jokes.text: Hey, clown, we've heard your stand-up! Now how about some shut-up!
jokes.setup: Audience tired of Krusty's bad jokes
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay heckle, economical and clear

jokes.text: Everybody's a comedian. / Except you!
jokes.setup: Krusty's defensive comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing on the comeback

jokes.text: Check it out. Two train wrecks connected by a strip of leather.
jokes.setup: Moe describing what he sees
jokes.punchline: The creative metaphor for ugly shoes/feet
jokes.explanation: Vivid, original imagery with good economy

jokes.text: I don't wanna say it's ugly, but PETA called and said we should kill this one.
jokes.setup: Moe pretending to be diplomatic about ugliness
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect misdirection structure

jokes.text: Even the Koreans wouldn't touch this dog!
jokes.setup: Moe commenting on the ugliest dog
jokes.explanation: Relies on ethnic stereotype, less sophisticated than other material

jokes.text: Humble faces, kids! Humbler! Lisa, too humble.
jokes.setup: Homer coaching kids on proper victory behavior
jokes.punchline: The escalating demands ending with Lisa overdoing it
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect character-specific ending

jokes.text: Wow, they love me for my bile. And I got a spleenful!
jokes.setup: Moe realizing people enjoy his meanness
jokes.explanation: Clever anatomical wordplay that fits Moe's character perfectly

jokes.text: No, no, no, I don't need your fair-weather friendship... is what I'll say tomorrow morning!
jokes.setup: Moe seeming to reject newfound popularity
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's just putting on an act for later
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing Moe's desperation for acceptance

jokes.text: Can you make it sound like girls askin' me? / Oh, Moe, please do it! Oh! Please? Moe, please? / Okay, you crazy dames, I'll do it!
jokes.setup: Moe wanting to pretend he's popular with women
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing both characters' desperation

jokes.text: I'll have to go home and drink better beer at half the price in natural lighting!
jokes.setup: Barney complaining about bar closing
jokes.punchline: Listing objective advantages of drinking at home as complaints
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic observation about bar culture and alcoholic logic

jokes.text: Yeah, that's right! Scatter, ya cockroaches!
jokes.setup: Moe turning off lights to force customers out
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with vivid imagery

jokes.text: I can see that down deep is a decent man.
jokes.setup: Girl Scout responding to Moe's mean face
jokes.punchline: Her impossible optimism about Moe's character
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast with Moe's actual character

jokes.text: Oh, can't a guy rush home from work to see his beautiful... / Moe's is closed, huh? / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Homer seeming romantic and devoted
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately seeing through his act
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent character knowledge between spouses

jokes.text: Even in the thought bubble, I gotta buy band candy?
jokes.setup: Homer imagining being at Moe's
jokes.punchline: The Girl Scout appearing even in his fantasy
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor about the medium itself

jokes.text: Looks like you ate a Rastafarian, and he's comin' back up on ya!
jokes.setup: Moe commenting on someone's beard
jokes.explanation: Gross but vivid imagery with good specificity

jokes.text: You talk like my ass plays harmonica.
jokes.setup: Moe responding to pretentious speech
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd and economical Moe-ism with great quotability

jokes.text: After the title page, it's Bob Dole's memoirs.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he's giving up on a book
jokes.punchline: The specific boring political memoir reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with dated but apt reference

jokes.text: Can I have another corpse? / They weren't corpses. / Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Moe casually asking for another corpse
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor escalation with economic structure

jokes.text: you're mean, you're funny and you're not British!
jokes.setup: Agent explaining why Moe is perfect for TV
jokes.explanation: Good cultural observation about reality TV judges

jokes.text: Don't let this pasty face and bad teeth fool ya.
jokes.setup: Moe insisting he's not British
jokes.punchline: Listing stereotypically British features he has
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about Moe's appearance

jokes.text: America's Ripest Bananas, So You Think You Can Judge, Who Wants to be a Welder? Poodle Versus Elephant... Leg Swap, Old People Try to Figure Out Computers
jokes.setup: Agent listing reality shows
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist list with good escalation, though long

jokes.text: Did you just say 'Armenian Idol'? 'Cause that's my favorite show!
jokes.setup: Agent mentioning American Idol
jokes.punchline: Moe mishearing and having oddly specific preferences
jokes.explanation: Good character quirk with specific cultural reference

jokes.text: I mixed all the spices together into one super spice!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his kitchen 'organization'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - trying to help but making things worse

jokes.text: I think you want the gay bar across the street.
jokes.setup: Question about XFL teams
jokes.explanation: Relies on stereotypes, not particularly clever

jokes.text: It's like somebody stepped on New York and scraped it off on the beach.
jokes.setup: Moe describing Los Angeles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, original metaphor with perfect economy and imagery

jokes.text: They charge you for parts and labor? Pick one, buddy.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about plumbers
jokes.explanation: Good character logic showing Homer's unreasonable cheapness

jokes.text: At least in hell, the heat still works.
jokes.setup: Discussing having husband around constantly
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery with unexpected darkness from Ned

jokes.text: I'm here, I'm gone. I'm here, I'm gone. I'm here. I get it. I get it. That-That's your thing.
jokes.setup: Simon demonstrating his ninja-like movement
jokes.punchline: Moe getting annoyed at the repetitive demonstration
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction with Moe's impatience at pretentious behavior

jokes.text: Well, that was a truly a remarkable version of 'Happy Birthday.' Because when it was done, I actually felt like I had lost a year of my life.
jokes.setup: Simon seeming to give positive feedback
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection structure with perfect Simon Cowell character voice

jokes.text: you should be on Broadway chargin' a hundred bucks a ticket.
jokes.setup: Moe being unexpectedly positive
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal showing Moe attempting positivity

jokes.text: But even more objectionable were Moe's insipid comments, a desperate plea for affection, which fell as flat as the hair on my head.
jokes.setup: Simon criticizing Moe's judging
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insult with self-awareness

jokes.text: Why would I try to sabotage a new judge who just might do what I do for $48 million less?
jokes.setup: Moe accusing Simon of sabotage
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on celebrity economics

jokes.text: I feel like the Tiger Woods of sex. In the mood for another round?
jokes.setup: Homer's post-coital confidence
jokes.explanation: Decent double entendre but fairly obvious wordplay

jokes.text: Legally, I can't say. / To a drunk man, that's a yes.
jokes.setup: Barney asking if he's okay to drive
jokes.explanation: Excellent drunk logic with perfect character integration

jokes.text: There is one bright side: I'm also forbidden from ever watching Fox. / You can't even show it in the bar? / That's right, and business has never been better.
jokes.setup: Moe's ban from California extending to Fox network
jokes.punchline: His bar improving without Fox programming
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about the show's own network

jokes.text: The president says Iran has gotten ahold of the most dangerous weapon known to man: The BP oil rig. That's right, ladies and gentlemen. But I know how to make that leak disappear: put it on NBC.
jokes.setup: Leno's topical political joke setup
jokes.explanation: Standard late-night TV joke structure with topical references

tmdb_overview: When Moe discovers a hidden talent for judging contests, he is approached by a television agent to join the AMERICAN IDOL judges' panel. Moe flies to Los Angeles where he tours the Fox lot and receives some sage career advice. Meanwhile, Homer drives Marge crazy when he begins hanging around the house.
